provenance: SYNTHETIC — authored for the eval harness; not real people or real résumés.

results.text:
Jordan Avery 
Backend Software Engineer 

I am a backend engineer with 6 years of experience building high-throughput services in Go and Python. I focus on reliability, latency, and cost, particularly in payment and search infrastructure.

**Experience** 

**Senior Backend Engineer, Northwind Payments** (2021-Present) 
I rebuilt the settlement pipeline in Go. This reduced end-of-day reconciliation from 40 minutes to 6 minutes. 
I reduced p99 checkout latency by 38% through a Redis read-through cache and connection pooling. 
I led the migration of 22 services from a monolith to Kubernetes without any customer-facing downtime. 
As an on-call rotation owner, I lowered incident MTTR from 55 to 18 minutes by improving runbooks. 

**Backend Engineer, Cedar Analytics** (2018-2021) 
I built an event-ingestion service in Python (FastAPI) that handled 12k events per second. 
I designed a PostgreSQL sharding scheme that supported 4x data growth. 
I created a CI pipeline using GitHub Actions to cut deploy time from 30 to 8 minutes. 

**Education** 
B.S. in Computer Science, State University (2018) 

**Skills** 
Go, Python, PostgreSQL, Redis, Kubernetes, Docker, gRPC, FastAPI, AWS, distributed systems, observability, CI/CD

I’ve always focused on improving the reliability and latency of production systems. At Northwind Payments, I rebuilt the settlement pipeline in Go, which significantly cut end-of-day reconciliation from 40 minutes to just 6 minutes. 

I also tackled checkout latency, reducing p99 times by 38% through a Redis read-through cache and connection pooling. This had a direct impact on user experience.

During my time leading the migration of 22 services from a monolith to Kubernetes, I ensured zero customer-facing downtime. This project required meticulous planning and execution to maintain reliability throughout. 

I took on the on-call rotation, where I drove incident mean time to resolution down from 55 to 18 minutes by improving our runbooks. Each of these experiences has strengthened my ability to create robust, efficient backend systems.

**Priya Raman** 
**Data Analyst** 

I'm a data analyst with 4 years of experience converting product and marketing data into actionable insights. I excel in SQL, Python (pandas), and dashboarding tools like Looker.

**Experience** 

**Data Analyst, Brightleaf Commerce** (2022-present) 
At Brightleaf, I built a self-serve funnel dashboard in Looker, utilized daily by 40 team members across product and growth. I also ran an A/B test that successfully increased checkout conversion by 11% through a simpler shipping process. Additionally, I streamlined our reporting cycle, reducing it from 2 days to just 3 hours by transitioning hand-built spreadsheets into dbt models.

**Junior Data Analyst, Harbor Media** (2020-2022) 
In my role at Harbor Media, I developed SQL queries for subscriber-retention analysis that informed our quarterly board deck. I also automated a churn-flag report using Python, creating a tool that the retention team continues to rely on.

**Education** 
B.A. Economics, City College (2020) 

**Skills** 
I am proficient in SQL, Python, pandas, dbt, Looker, Tableau, A/B testing, statistics, data visualization, and ETL. I specialize in product and growth analytics, with a focus on experimentation and improving conversion rates.

I have spent the last four years as a data analyst, transforming product and marketing data into actionable insights. I am fluent in SQL, Python with pandas, and various dashboarding tools.

Currently, as a Data Analyst at Brightleaf Commerce, I built a self-serve funnel dashboard in Looker. This dashboard is currently used by 40 team members across product and growth daily. I also ran an A/B test that increased checkout conversion by 11% by streamlining the shipping process. Through this experience, I learned how to utilize data to directly influence product decisions. Additionally, I improved our reporting efficiency significantly by reducing the cycle from two days to just three hours through dbt modeling.

Earlier, in my role as a Junior Data Analyst at Harbor Media, I wrote SQL queries for subscriber-retention analyses that contributed to our quarterly board deck. I also automated a churn-flag report in Python, which continues to support our retention efforts.

I hold a B.A. in Economics from City College, completed in 2020. My skills include SQL, Python, pandas, dbt, Looker, Tableau, A/B testing, and data visualization. I am committed to leveraging data analytics to drive product and growth decisions.

**Marcus Bell** 
**Marketing Manager** 

I bring 8 years of experience in B2B SaaS marketing. My focus is on demand generation, content, and lifecycle programs. I take ownership of pipeline contribution and customer acquisition cost. 

**Experience** 

**Marketing Manager, Summit CRM** (2019-present) 
I grew the marketing-sourced pipeline by 62% in two years. This success came from rebuilding our webinar and content program. I also reduced the cost per marketing-qualified lead by 28% by reallocating spend from paid search to lifecycle email. I manage a team of 4 and oversee a $1.2M annual budget. 

**Demand Generation Specialist, Orbit Software** (2015-2019) 
In this role, I ran paid social and search campaigns that produced 30% of new-trial signups. I collaborated with the sales team to build the lead-scoring model in HubSpot. 

**Education** 
B.A. Communications, Lakeside University (2015) 

**Skills** 
Demand generation, content marketing, marketing automation, HubSpot, Marketo, SEO, paid media, lifecycle email, analytics, team leadership.

I have extensive experience in growing marketing-sourced pipeline within the B2B SaaS sector. As the Marketing Manager at Summit CRM, I led the charge to rebuild the webinar and content program. This effort resulted in a 62% increase in our marketing-sourced pipeline over just two years.

Additionally, I focused on optimizing our spending to enhance efficiency. By reallocating our resources from paid search to lifecycle email, I successfully reduced our cost per marketing-qualified lead by 28%. My strategic approach ensured we added significant value to our pipeline while managing a team of four and a $1.2M budget. 

Before Summit CRM, I worked as a Demand Generation Specialist at Orbit Software. There, I ran paid social and search campaigns, which contributed to 30% of new-trial signups. I also collaborated with the sales team to build a lead-scoring model in HubSpot, which further streamlined our pipeline management. 

With eight years in B2B SaaS marketing, I am committed to driving demand generation, content, and lifecycle marketing efforts that deliver measurable results.

Elena Torres, RN

As a registered nurse with 7 years of experience, I specialize in critical care. My background includes extensive work in both medical-surgical and intensive-care units. I am certified in BLS and ACLS.

Currently, I serve as an ICU registered nurse at Riverside General Hospital. I manage the care of up to 3 critically ill patients per shift, including those on ventilators and post-op cardiac cases. I also precept new-graduate nurses, having onboarded 9 over the past three years. As a charge nurse on two shifts a week, I coordinate staffing and admissions for a busy 24-bed unit.

Previously, I worked as a Med-Surg registered nurse at Valley Health. There, I cared for 5 to 6 post-surgical patients per shift with a documented zero medication error record. I played a key role in rolling out a barcode medication-administration system on the floor.

I hold a B.S. in Nursing from State University, earned in 2017. My active RN license reflects my commitment to patient care and my expertise in critical care, medical-surgical nursing, and care coordination.

I am a registered nurse with 7 years of experience in medical-surgical and intensive-care units. Currently, I work as an ICU Registered Nurse at Riverside General Hospital. Here, I manage care for up to 3 critically ill patients each shift. This includes caring for patients on ventilators and those recovering from cardiac surgeries.

I have a strong commitment to teaching and mentoring. Over the past three years, I've precepted nine new-graduate nurses, helping them adapt to the fast-paced ICU environment. In addition, I serve as a charge nurse for two shifts each week. This role involves coordinating staffing and admissions for our 24-bed unit, ensuring efficient patient flow and care delivery.

My previous experience at Valley Health as a Med-Surg Registered Nurse further solidified my skills. I cared for 5 to 6 post-surgical patients per shift. I am proud of my documented zero medication-error record during this time. I also contributed to the team that rolled out the barcode medication-administration system, enhancing patient safety.

I hold a B.S. in Nursing from State University and maintain active certifications in BLS and ACLS. My experience has equipped me with expertise in critical care, patient assessment, care coordination, and IV therapy. I am dedicated to providing high-quality care to my patients.

I have a solid foundation in experimentation and analysis, particularly in e-commerce. My work focuses on actionable recommendations that drive results.

In my experience with A/B testing, I prioritize statistical rigor to ensure valid results. I begin by clearly defining the hypothesis and objectives. This gives structure to the test. I also determine the target sample size, using power analysis to minimize error.

Randomization is key. I randomize participants to eliminate bias in the groups. While running the test, I monitor data quality. This includes checking for confounding variables that might skew results. 

Once the test concludes, I analyze the data with attention to statistical significance. P-values help me gauge the strength of the findings. I often present these insights visually, making it easier for stakeholders to understand the outcomes.

I ensure that any conclusions drawn from the A/B tests are actionable and informed by the data, serving to enhance decision-making in product and marketing strategies.
